title: griselinia lucida
intro: Griselinia lucida, also known as New Zealand Spinach or Kapuka, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the Griselinia genus. The leaves and shoots of Griselinia lucida have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. Griselinia lucida is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in cooking as a spinach substitute, with its leaves rich in vitamins and minerals.
